content.body:
## Epic Description
Stabilize current production by clearing three open bugs (B1 access-code CTA, B1 route separation, B2 modal icon centering) and lay the foundation for the v2 image pipeline by deciding between ImageSharp and Magick.NET, building a watermark service, caching watermarked variants, and serving them by default in the photo modal. The watermark pipeline established here is reused throughout v2 (downloads, sharing, previews).

## User Stories
- [B1] Add "Have an access code?" CTA on home/login page
- [B1] Audit `/albums/:id` vs `/code/:code` route separation
- [B2] Fix photo modal icon centering CSS
- [B3] Decide image processing library (ImageSharp vs Magick.NET) ΓÇö ADR
- [B3] Build watermark service
- [B3] Cache watermarked variants in storage
- [B3] PhotoModal serves watermarked URL by default

## Dependencies
None. Foundational ΓÇö gates EPIC-05 (pricing) and EPIC-06 (cart/checkout) implicitly because watermarked previews underlie the "preview vs purchase" UX.

## Acceptance Criteria
- **Given** an unauthenticated visitor, **When** they land on home/login, **Then** they see an "Have an access code?" CTA that routes to `/code/{code}`.
- **Given** the route audit is complete, **When** an admin visits `/albums/:id`, **Then** admin UI is shown; **When** a guest with code visits `/code/:code`, **Then** guest UI is shown with no admin actions.
- **Given** the photo modal is open, **When** any user inspects close/prev/next, **Then** glyphs are visually centered.
- **Given** the team needs server-side watermarks, **When** the ADR is merged, **Then** `Documentation/architecture/adr/` contains the library decision and `PhotoGallery.csproj` carries the chosen package.
- **Given** a guest opens a photo in the modal, **When** the image loads, **Then** it is the watermarked variant; **When** they purchase and download, **Then** they receive the unwatermarked original.

## Risks
- Watermark removal arms race ΓÇö anti-removal techniques are imperfect; document threat model.
- Pipeline library choice locks downstream features (face-blur, EXIF strip, format conversions).
- Magick.NET native dependency may complicate Linux container images.
content.title: [EPIC-01] Bug hotfix + image pipeline foundation (size: S)

items.title: [EPIC-01] Bug hotfix + image pipeline foundation (size: S)

content.body:
## Epic Description
Introduce first-class Registered Users on top of the existing IDP-only auth model (no local passwords). A registered user can link guest access codes to their account, see their Shared Albums dashboard, and create their own albums. Stand up transactional email via Azure Communication Services (verification, password-reset-not-applicable, GDPR notifications, share invites). Pull profile images from Gravatar by default. Implement GDPR data export and account deletion.

## User Stories
- F2: Linked Code Accounts (guest code ΓåÆ registered user)
- F2: "Shared Albums" dashboard listing albums the user has codes/grants for
- F2: Registered users can create their own albums
- Email infra: Azure Communication Services SDK + sender domain configuration
- Email verification on first login (link IDP email ΓåÆ User row)
- GDPR: data export (JSON) and account deletion endpoints
- Profile image: Gravatar fallback by email hash

## Dependencies
EPIC-01 (foundational image pipeline so registered users have a working modal).

## Acceptance Criteria
- **Given** a guest with a working code, **When** they sign up via an IDP, **Then** their existing code-based access is linked to the new User record.
- **Given** a registered user, **When** they view their dashboard, **Then** they see "My Albums" and "Shared Albums" sections.
- **Given** a registered user requests data export, **When** the request completes, **Then** they receive an email with a JSON export of their data.
- **Given** ACS is configured, **When** any verification email is triggered, **Then** the email is delivered from the configured sender domain.
- **Given** a user has no custom profile image, **When** their avatar renders, **Then** Gravatar is used.

## Risks
- ACS sender domain verification is a manual Azure step ΓÇö block on tenant admin.
- GDPR delete must cascade across photos, codes, albums ΓÇö high blast radius.
- IDP email mismatch (Google account email != Microsoft account email) handled in EPIC-02b.
content.title: [EPIC-02] Registered Users + Azure Communication Services Email (size: M)

items.title: [EPIC-02] Registered Users + Azure Communication Services Email (size: M)

content.body:
## Context
Guests with an access code currently have no obvious entry point from the home/login page.

## Acceptance Criteria
- **Given** an unauthenticated visitor, **When** they land on home/login, **Then** they see a button "Have an access code? Enter it ΓåÆ".
- **Given** the visitor clicks the CTA, **When** they enter a code, **Then** they are redirected to `/code/{code}`.
- **Given** an invalid code, **When** they submit, **Then** they see a clear error.

## Files likely affected
- `FE.PhotoGallery/src/app/components/login/login.component.ts`
- `FE.PhotoGallery/src/app/components/login/login.component.html`

## Dependencies
None.

## Out of scope
- Refactoring the login component layout beyond adding the CTA.
- Server-side code-validation changes.

Part of #1
content.title: [B1] Add "Have an access code?" CTA on home/login page

items.title: [B1] Add "Have an access code?" CTA on home/login page

content.body:
## Epic Description
When a user signs in with a *new* IDP whose email matches an existing User, prompt for an email-verification code to link the new external identity rather than creating a duplicate account. Backed by a new `UserExternalIdentity` table. Includes rate limiting, code expiry, attempt limits, race protection during user creation, and an Account Settings panel showing all linked identities.

## User Stories
- New `UserExternalIdentity` table (UserId, Provider, Subject, Email, LinkedAt)
- Detect "email exists, IDP subject does not" on login ΓåÆ trigger email verification flow
- Email verification code: 6-digit, 10-minute expiry, 3 attempts max
- Rate limit: 5 codes per email per hour
- Race protection: per-email lock during user creation to prevent duplicate Users
- Account Settings page showing linked identities + ability to unlink (cannot unlink last one)

## Dependencies
EPIC-02 (Registered Users + ACS Email).

## Acceptance Criteria
- **Given** a User with email `x@y.com` linked to Google, **When** they log in via Microsoft with the same email, **Then** they receive a 6-digit code at `x@y.com` and on entry the Microsoft identity is added to `UserExternalIdentity`.
- **Given** 5 codes already issued in the last hour for an email, **When** a 6th attempt is made, **Then** the request is rate-limited with a clear error.
- **Given** two simultaneous first-time logins for the same email, **When** they race, **Then** exactly one User row is created (verified by integration test).
- **Given** a User with one linked identity, **When** they try to unlink it, **Then** the operation is rejected.

## Risks
- Race protection requires either DB unique constraint + retry or distributed lock.
- IDP that does not return a verified email (rare) ΓÇö must be blocked from auto-link.
content.title: [EPIC-02b] Multi-IDP Account Linking (size: S/M)

items.title: [EPIC-02b] Multi-IDP Account Linking (size: S/M)

content.body:
## Context
Admin and guest flows share UI shapes too closely; URL shape mismatch is not consistently rejected.

## Acceptance Criteria
- **Given** an admin, **When** they visit `/albums/:id`, **Then** they see admin UI (upload, delete, generate codes).
- **Given** a guest with a valid code, **When** they visit `/code/:code`, **Then** they see guest UI only ΓÇö no admin actions.
- **Given** an unauthenticated visitor, **When** they hit `/albums/:id`, **Then** they are redirected to login.
- **Given** an admin URL is opened by a non-admin user, **When** the route resolves, **Then** access is denied / redirected.

## Files likely affected
- `FE.PhotoGallery/src/app/components/albums/album-detail.component.ts`
- `FE.PhotoGallery/src/app/components/code-gallery/code-gallery.component.ts`
- Route guards in `FE.PhotoGallery/src/app/`

## Dependencies
None.

## Out of scope
- Backend permission model changes (covered by EPIC-03).

Part of #1

Close / prev / next button glyphs render off-center inside their circular hit-areas.

## Acceptance Criteria
- **Given** the photo modal is open, **When** any user inspects close/prev/next buttons, **Then** glyphs are visually centered horizontally and vertically inside their circles across desktop and mobile.

content.body:
## Context
Server-side watermarking is on the immediate roadmap (subsequent stories in EPIC-01). We need a library decision before writing the watermark service.

## Acceptance Criteria
- **Given** the team needs to add watermarks server-side, **When** the decision is made, **Then** there is a written ADR in `Documentation/architecture/adr/` documenting library choice + reasoning.
- **Given** the ADR is merged, **When** `PhotoGallery.csproj` is inspected, **Then** the chosen package reference is present.

## Decision inputs
- **ImageSharp**: managed code, simpler MIT-equivalent (Six Labors Split License ΓÇö confirm commercial terms), good performance, no native deps.
- **Magick.NET**: native ImageMagick under the hood, broader format support, license restrictions for commercial use to vet.
- Consult the architect skill for the final call.

## Files likely affected
- `Documentation/architecture/adr/NNNN-image-processing-library.md` (new)
- `PhotoGallery/PhotoGallery.csproj`

## Dependencies
None ΓÇö gates B3-watermark-service.

## Out of scope
- Implementing the watermark service itself (separate story).
- Refactoring existing image-processing code.

Part of #1
content.title: [B3] Decide image processing library (ImageSharp vs Magick.NET)

items.title: [B3] Decide image processing library (ImageSharp vs Magick.NET)

content.body:
## Context
Watermarked Medium-quality variants will be served as the public preview throughout v2 (cart, photo modal, share links). Watermark must resist basic AI removal.

## Acceptance Criteria
- **Given** a Medium-quality variant is generated during processing, **When** the watermark service runs, **Then** the resulting image has a semi-transparent watermark overlay positioned over the subject (not corner-only).
- **Given** the watermark is applied, **When** it is reviewed against a small AI-removal test set, **Then** removal artifacts are visible (we do not require unbreakable ΓÇö we require not-trivially-removable).
- **Given** unit tests for the service, **When** they run, **Then** they verify input/output dimensions and watermark presence.

## Files likely affected
- New: `PhotoGallery/Services/WatermarkService.cs`
- New: `PhotoGallery/Services/IWatermarkService.cs`
- DI registration in `Program.cs`

## Dependencies
- Depends on B3-decision (library choice ADR).

## Out of scope
- Caching watermarked variants in storage (separate story).
- Wiring into the photo modal (separate story).

Part of #1
content.title: [B3] Build watermark service

items.title: [B3] Build watermark service

content.body:
## Context
Re-rendering the watermark on every request is wasteful. The watermarked Medium variant should be persisted alongside other quality variants and served from storage.

## Acceptance Criteria
- **Given** a photo finishes processing, **When** the watermarked variant is generated, **Then** it is persisted as a separate quality (e.g., `MediumWatermarked`) or transformation cache in the configured storage provider.
- **Given** a subsequent request for the watermarked variant, **When** it is served, **Then** it comes from cache without re-rendering.
- **Given** the original photo is deleted, **When** the cleanup runs, **Then** the watermarked variant is also removed.

## Files likely affected
- `PhotoGallery/Services/Processing/ImageProcessingService.cs`
- `PhotoGallery/Models/PhotoFile.cs` (if a new quality enum value or column is required)
- EF migration if schema changes

## Dependencies
- Depends on B3-watermark-service.

## Out of scope
- Serving the watermarked URL from the modal (next story).

Part of #1
content.title: [B3] Cache watermarked variants in storage

items.title: [B3] Cache watermarked variants in storage

content.body:
## Context
Guests must see the watermarked preview in the modal. Buyers receive the unwatermarked original on download after purchase.

## Acceptance Criteria
- **Given** a guest views a photo in the modal, **When** the image loads, **Then** it is the watermarked Medium variant ΓÇö not the original.
- **Given** a user purchases the photo, **When** they download it from the cart, **Then** they receive the unwatermarked original at the selected quality.
- **Given** the backend DTO returning `mediumUrl`, **When** inspected, **Then** it points to the watermarked variant for guest contexts and the original for purchased contexts.

## Files likely affected
- `FE.PhotoGallery/src/app/components/photo-modal/photo-modal.component.ts`
- Backend DTOs returning `mediumUrl` (likely `PhotoGallery/Models/Dtos/` or controller projection)

## Dependencies
- Depends on B3-watermark-cache.

## Out of scope
- Cart/checkout download flow (covered by EPIC-06).

Part of #1
content.title: [B3] PhotoModal serves watermarked URL by default

items.title: [B3] PhotoModal serves watermarked URL by default

content.body:
## Epic Description
Replace the binary owner/guest model with per-(album, user) grants of View / Modify / Admin. Add album artwork (auto-pick first photo, customizable by Modify+ users). Stand up the audit log scaffold that all later epics will consume (payments, admin console, moderation). Support transferring ownership. Owners grant access by entering a *registered* user's email ΓÇö there is no invitation/onboarding-via-share flow.

## User Stories
- F3: AlbumPermission entity (AlbumId, UserId, Level: View|Modify|Admin)
- F3: Grant/revoke UI (owner + Admin only)
- F3: Authorization policy enforcing per-album level
- F5: Album artwork = first photo by default; Modify+ can upload custom
- Audit log scaffold: `AuditEvent` table (Actor, Action, Target, Timestamp, Metadata JSON)
- Transfer ownership flow (current owner ΓåÆ another User; downgrades old owner to Admin)
- Owner grant by email: must match an existing registered user; error otherwise

## Dependencies
EPIC-02 (registered users must exist before they can be granted permissions).

## Acceptance Criteria
- **Given** an album owner, **When** they grant View to `bob@example.com`, **Then** Bob's User row exists OR the grant is rejected with "user must register first".
- **Given** a Modify-level user, **When** they upload custom album artwork, **Then** it replaces the auto first-photo artwork.
- **Given** any permission change, **When** it is persisted, **Then** an `AuditEvent` row is written.
- **Given** an owner transfers ownership, **When** the transfer completes, **Then** the old owner is now Admin and the new owner has full Owner rights.

## Risks
- Audit log volume ΓÇö define retention from day one.
- Permission cache invalidation across nodes (if/when we scale out).
content.title: [EPIC-03] Album Permissions + audit log (size: M)

items.title: [EPIC-03] Album Permissions + audit log (size: M)

content.body:
## Epic Description
Build a provider-agnostic payment subsystem that can plug Stripe / PayPal / Amazon Pay later. Define `IPaymentProvider` (factory pattern matching `IExternalTokenValidator` / `IStorageProvider`), ship a `MockPaymentProvider` backed by EF entities (`MockBalance`, `MockTransaction` ΓÇö append-only ledger), and add admin endpoints to credit fake funds, view ledger, and simulate webhooks. Schema is provider-agnostic from day one: `PaymentMethod`, `Order`, `OrderLineItem`, `Charge`, `Refund`, `Payout` ΓÇö every money column carries an explicit `Currency` (default USD).

## User Stories
- `IPaymentProvider` interface + factory (DI-resolved by config key)
- `MockPaymentProvider` implementation
- EF entities: `MockBalance`, `MockTransaction` (append-only)
- EF entities: `PaymentMethod`, `Order`, `OrderLineItem`, `Charge`, `Refund`, `Payout` with `Currency` columns
- `IdempotencyKey` UNIQUE constraint on Charge/Refund
- Admin endpoints: `POST /admin/mock-payments/credit`, `GET /admin/mock-payments/ledger`, `POST /admin/mock-payments/simulate-webhook`
- Reserve enum slots for Stripe/PayPal/AmazonPay

## Dependencies
EPIC-02 (User must exist to own PaymentMethods).

## Acceptance Criteria
- **Given** the mock provider is configured, **When** an admin credits $50 to a user, **Then** a `MockTransaction` row is appended and `MockBalance` reflects the new balance.
- **Given** a duplicate charge with the same idempotency key, **When** it is submitted, **Then** the second request returns the original Charge (no double-charge).
- **Given** any money column in the schema, **When** inspected, **Then** it has a sibling `Currency` column with `USD` default.
- **Given** Stripe is later swapped in, **When** the config flag flips, **Then** no schema changes are required.

## Risks
- Designing the abstraction too narrowly to fit Stripe ergonomics ΓÇö review with architect before merge.
- Idempotency semantics must align with real provider conventions.
content.title: [EPIC-04] Payment Provider Abstraction + Mock Provider (size: M)

items.title: [EPIC-04] Payment Provider Abstraction + Mock Provider (size: M)

content.body:
## Epic Description
Sellers must have a verified payment method on file before they can upload photos. Introduce a `CanUpload` ASP.NET Core authorization policy (true iff user has ΓëÑ1 verified `PaymentMethod`). Apply it to `PhotoUploadController`. UI: disable the upload button with a tooltip when ungated; dashboard empty-state prompts the user to add a payment method.

## User Stories
- `CanUpload` authorization policy + handler
- Apply policy to `PhotoUploadController` (or upload endpoints)
- FE: upload button disabled state with tooltip "Add a payment method to upload"
- FE: dashboard empty-state CTA ΓåÆ payment method setup

## Dependencies
EPIC-04 (payment method entity must exist).

## Acceptance Criteria
- **Given** a registered user with no PaymentMethod, **When** they POST to upload, **Then** the response is 403 with a clear reason.
- **Given** the same user, **When** they view the dashboard, **Then** an empty-state prompts them to link a payment method.
- **Given** they link a verified PaymentMethod, **When** they retry upload, **Then** it succeeds.

## Risks
- During mock-only phase, "verified" semantics need a definition (mock auto-verifies).
content.title: [EPIC-04b] Upload gated on linked payment method (size: S)

items.title: [EPIC-04b] Upload gated on linked payment method (size: S)

content.body:
## Epic Description
Per-photo pricing (free or $N), with bulk-set, default-on-upload, and per-album default. Introduce a platform fee charged on both upload and download (per MB). Stand up an admin Settings page shell (admin-only) where the platform fee schedule lives. Pricing must be **snapshotted** at order-add time so price history does not retroactively change open carts or completed orders.

## User Stories
- F1: Photo.Price (nullable decimal, USD), Photo.IsFree
- F1: Bulk-set price across selected photos
- F1: Default-price-on-upload (per album default + global default)
- F4: Platform fee schedule (per MB, upload + download), admin-configurable
- Settings page shell: `/admin/settings` (admin-only)
- Order line items snapshot price + platform fee at add-to-cart time

## Dependencies
EPIC-04 (Order/OrderLineItem schema).

## Acceptance Criteria
- **Given** a Modify+ user on an album, **When** they bulk-set 20 photos to $2, **Then** all 20 reflect the new price.
- **Given** a photo at $5 added to a cart, **When** the seller raises the price to $7, **Then** the existing cart line stays at $5.
- **Given** an admin updates the per-MB upload fee, **When** the next upload completes, **Then** the new fee is applied.

## Risks
- Currency assumptions ΓÇö explicitly USD in v2; revisit for multi-currency in v3.
- Platform fee math edge cases (rounding) ΓÇö pin to banker's rounding with tests.
content.title: [EPIC-05] Photo Pricing + Platform Fees (size: M)

items.title: [EPIC-05] Photo Pricing + Platform Fees (size: M)

content.body:
## Epic Description
End-to-end purchase flow: multi-album cart, persistent cart for registered users, visual cart indicators (border on selected photos, button toggle), quality selection at checkout (Low / Medium / High / Original / All). Coupon system: N free downloads, percent off, max uses, atomic DB enforcement (no double-spend), mandatory expiry. Special "platform-fee-waiver" coupons that require app-owner approval workflow. Coupon redemption is rate-limited per IP.

## User Stories
- Multi-album persistent cart (registered users) + session cart (guests)
- Visual selection indicators (photo border, "Add/Remove" toggle)
- Checkout quality selector
- Coupon entity: code, type (free-N / percent-off / fee-waiver), max uses, used count, expires_at (NOT NULL)
- Atomic redemption: `UPDATE ... WHERE used_count < max_uses` returning rowcount
- Fee-waiver coupons require app-owner approval before activation
- IP-based rate limiting on coupon redemption endpoint

## Dependencies
EPIC-04 (payment provider) + EPIC-05 (pricing).

## Acceptance Criteria
- **Given** a user adds photos from albums A and B, **When** they checkout, **Then** both albums' photos appear in one Order.
- **Given** a coupon with max_uses=10, **When** 11 concurrent redemptions race, **Then** exactly 10 succeed.
- **Given** a coupon with no expiry, **When** an admin tries to save it, **Then** the save is rejected.
- **Given** a fee-waiver coupon is created, **When** an app-owner has not approved it, **Then** redemptions fail.
- **Given** an IP submits >N redemptions/min, **When** the threshold trips, **Then** further attempts are 429.

## Risks
- Cart persistence model (DB vs Redis) ΓÇö pick one and document.
- Coupon abuse via IP rotation ΓÇö mitigate with both IP and user rate limit.
- Quality-selection at checkout interacts with watermark cache (EPIC-01).
content.title: [EPIC-06] Cart, Checkout, Coupons (size: L)

items.title: [EPIC-06] Cart, Checkout, Coupons (size: L)

content.body:
## Epic Description
Operator console for the app owner: user management (disable, list, role change), all-albums view across the platform, expanded settings page, admin audit log UI (consumes the EPIC-03 scaffold), and a moderation surface (flag/take-down on photos and albums).

## User Stories
- F4: User management (list, search, disable, role assign)
- F4: All-albums view (cross-tenant)
- Settings page expansion (platform fees from EPIC-05, coupon approval queue from EPIC-06)
- Admin audit log UI: filter by actor/action/target/date
- Moderation: flag / take-down actions on photos/albums

## Dependencies
EPIC-02 (users) + EPIC-05 (settings shell).

## Acceptance Criteria
- **Given** an app-owner, **When** they disable a user, **Then** that user can no longer log in and an audit event is recorded.
- **Given** the audit log UI, **When** the app-owner filters by actor and date, **Then** only matching events show.
- **Given** a flagged photo, **When** the app-owner takes it down, **Then** it is hidden from all guest/buyer views and the owner is notified.

## Risks
- Cross-tenant visibility implies sensitive data ΓÇö ensure role check is server-side.
- Moderation actions need an appeal/restore path.
content.title: [EPIC-07] App-Owner Admin Console (size: M)

items.title: [EPIC-07] App-Owner Admin Console (size: M)

content.body:
## Epic Description
Round out the user-facing account experience: custom profile image upload (overrides Gravatar), payment method management UI, download preferences (default quality, etc.), and TOTP 2FA. 2FA is *gated* by has-payment-method-on-file (so we don't lock users out before they have anything to pay with).

## User Stories
- F6: Custom profile image upload
- F6: Payment method linkage UI (list, add, remove, set default)
- F6: Download preferences (default quality)
- TOTP 2FA enrollment + login challenge
- 2FA gating: only offered/required if user has ΓëÑ1 PaymentMethod

## Dependencies
EPIC-04 (PaymentMethod entity).

## Acceptance Criteria
- **Given** a user uploads a custom profile image, **When** their avatar renders anywhere, **Then** the custom image wins over Gravatar.
- **Given** a user with a PaymentMethod, **When** they enable TOTP, **Then** subsequent logins prompt for the code.
- **Given** a user with no PaymentMethod, **When** they visit security settings, **Then** TOTP enrollment is hidden or messaged accordingly.

## Risks
- 2FA recovery codes ΓÇö must be generated and shown once.
- Profile image abuse (NSFW) ΓÇö moderation surface from EPIC-07 covers this.
content.title: [EPIC-08] User Profile + 2FA + Payment Method UI (size: M)

items.title: [EPIC-08] User Profile + 2FA + Payment Method UI (size: M)

content.body:
## Epic Description
Cross-cutting hardening woven throughout the v2 phases: search/filter primitives, a reusable bulk-select UI primitive, EXIF stripping on upload, rate limiting middleware, and the audit log scaffold (initially defined in EPIC-03 and consumed everywhere). Stories slot into earlier phases as needed rather than landing as a single block.

## User Stories
- Search/filter on albums and photos (FE primitive + BE query)
- Bulk-select Angular primitive (used by pricing, permissions, cart)
- EXIF stripping pipeline step (location, camera serial)
- Global rate limiting middleware (auth, coupon, upload, payment)
- Audit log scaffold (already defined in EPIC-03; this epic tracks consumers)

## Dependencies
None standalone; stories block on whichever earlier epic surfaces them.

## Acceptance Criteria
- **Given** any uploaded photo, **When** processing completes, **Then** the stored variants contain no GPS EXIF.
- **Given** a list view (photos, users, audit), **When** the user types in the search field, **Then** results filter within 300ms.
- **Given** a brute-force login attempt, **When** the threshold trips, **Then** the IP is rate-limited.

## Risks
- EXIF stripping must not damage color profiles.
- Rate limit thresholds tuned by traffic data ΓÇö start conservative.
content.title: [EPIC-09] Operational Hardening (continuous) (size: M+)

items.title: [EPIC-09] Operational Hardening (continuous) (size: M+)

content.body:
Closes #14
Part of #1

## What
Center the close (├ù), prev (ΓÇ╣), and next (ΓÇ║) glyphs inside the circular buttons of PhotoModalComponent. Line-height alone was unreliable across fonts.

## How
- Switch to `display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center`
- Pin `font-family: Arial` for predictable glyph metrics
- Reduce font-size 28ΓåÆ24 (close) and 36ΓåÆ32 (nav) so heavier Arial glyphs fit cleanly

## Verify
Open photo modal in code-gallery or album-detail, confirm ├ù ΓÇ╣ ΓÇ║ are dead-center.
content.title: fix(B2): center icons in photo modal nav and close buttons

items.title: fix(B2): center icons in photo modal nav and close buttons

content.body:
Closes #12
Part of #1
Audit for #13 (route separation) included ΓÇö both /albums/:id and /code/:code routes already correctly separated by authGuard; no code change needed there.

## What
Adds an "Enter Access Code" CTA on the home/login page so unauthenticated guests with an access code can reach the public gallery without knowing the URL pattern. Per requirement #4.

## How
- LoginComponent imports FormsModule for ngModel binding
- Below the Google login + divider, a button reveals an inline form
- Client-side shape validation: alphanumeric+hyphen, 4-32 chars (server validates the actual code)
- Submit (Enter or click) navigates to `/code/{CODE}` (uppercase normalized)
- `data-testid` attributes added for upcoming Playwright coverage

content.body:
Closes #15, #16, #17, #18
Part of #1

## What
Server-rendered watermark pipeline so guests viewing the photo modal see a watermarked Medium variant instead of the unwatermarked image. Cart-checkout still delivers the unwatermarked Medium for paid users.

## ADR
`docs/decisions/D009-watermark-pipeline.md` ΓÇö chose to extend the existing SixLabors.ImageSharp v3 (already in project) rather than introduce Magick.NET. Flags the v3 commercial-license question for separate resolution before EPIC-04.

## How
- New `WatermarkService` (PhotoGallery/Services/WatermarkService.cs): tiled, semi-transparent diagonal text covering the entire image (subject overlap + repeating pattern + 30┬░ angle = harder to AI-remove than corner-only)
- `ImageProcessingService` runs `GenerateWatermarkedVariantAsync` after the Medium quality variant is encoded ΓåÆ `photogallery/{albumId}/{photoId}/medium-watermarked.jpg`
- `PhotoVersionUrlService.GenerateShortLivedUrlAsync` gains a `watermarked` parameter; when true and quality=Medium it serves the watermarked variant with graceful fallback to unwatermarked if not yet generated
- `AccessCodeController.GetAlbumPhotos` now passes `watermarked: true` so the public/code-based gallery serves the watermarked Medium

## Tests
- 7 new `WatermarkServiceTests`: valid JPEG output, pixel diff vs original, empty/long/null text, JPEG quality behavior
- 112/112 backend tests pass (105 + 7 new)

## Limits & Future Work
This is a **deterrent, not prevention** ΓÇö sufficiently determined users with state-of-the-art AI tools may still remove watermarks. Future EPIC: per-photographer watermark customization (logo upload).

## Verify
- Process a photo ΓåÆ check that `medium-watermarked.jpg` is created in MinIO
- Visit /code/<code> ΓåÆ photo previews + modal show watermarked Medium
- Cart checkout ΓåÆ ZIP contains unwatermarked Medium

content.body:
Closes #24
Part of #2

## What
GDPR-compliant data export and account hard-delete with cascade. Audit log scaffold reused by all later epics.

## How
- `AuditLogEntry` entity + repository + EF migration `AddAuditLogTable`
- `GdprService.ExportUserDataAsync` returns serializable `UserDataExport` (profile + owned albums + uploaded photos + saved codes + downloads); schemaVersion `1.0`
- `GdprService.DeleteUserAsync` performs cascade hard delete inside a relational transaction with explicit child-row removal (deterministic on SQLite and InMemory)
- Audit log entry written on deletion with denormalized `ActorEmail` so "who deleted whom" survives
- Storage cleanup deferred via `user.deletion.storage-pending` audit row carrying storage keys
- Both endpoints resolve userId strictly from JWT NameIdentifier (no impersonation surface)

## Tests
8 new GdprServiceTests covering happy path + security + cascade + audit + not-found. Full suite: 113/113 passing.

## Files
9 new + 3 modified (DbContext, Program.cs, snapshot)

content.body:
Closes #23
Part of #2

## What
Registered users can save access codes to their account and view all saved albums on /shared-albums. Codes survive between sessions across browsers.

## How
**Backend**
- `SavedAccessCode` entity with UNIQUE (UserId, AccessCodeId) index ΓÇö Cascade delete on AccessCode, NoAction on User
- EF migration `AddSavedAccessCodeTable`
- `AccountController` with `[Authorize]` `/api/account/access-codes`: GET (list user's saved), POST (idempotent save), DELETE (unlink, never deletes the AccessCode)
- Multi-tenancy security: every operation verifies UserId matches the JWT claim

**Frontend**
- "Save to my account" button on `/code/:code` (visible only when authed); inline Γ£ô Saved feedback
- New `/shared-albums` route + standalone component listing saved codes as cards (View Album / Remove buttons)
- Empty state guides user to /code/<their-code>

## Tests
7 new `SavedAccessCodeApiTests`: create/valid, idempotent, 404 invalid, 400 expired, list scoping (multi-tenancy), delete-unlinks-not-deletes-code, 403 cross-user delete. **112 / 112 passing**.

## Conflict note
This adds the **real** `/shared-albums` route. PR #26 (Slice D) adds a stub redirect for /shared-albums. When merging, drop Slice D's stub redirect ΓÇö Slice B's component is the real implementation.

content.body:
## What
`docker-compose up -d` was failing because it tried to build a broken backend container. Move backend+frontend behind a `full-container` profile so default compose only brings up MinIO/Postgres/Keycloak ΓÇö which is the dev workflow. Backend+frontend are run locally with `dotnet watch` + `ng serve` for hot-reload.

Fixed Dockerfile.backend COPY paths too, so `docker-compose --profile full-container up -d` works for CI smoke tests / full-container demos.

## Why
The `Full Stack: Start All Services` VS Code task was hitting:
`ERROR [backend build 3/6] COPY PhotoGallery.csproj .: not found`

This blocks anyone trying to test EPIC-01/02 features end-to-end via the standard task chain.

## Verified
`docker-compose up -d` brings up postgres, keycloak, minio cleanly (no backend build attempted).

content.body:
## What
Extract cross-cutting auth and configuration concerns into dedicated Class Library sub-projects per the **clean-architecture-guide** skill. PhotoGallery.csproj now references `Authentication.csproj` and `Configuration.csproj`; they don't reference back, which is **compile-enforced**.

## Why
Per VerdantIQ's pattern (referenced by user) ΓÇö cross-cutting concerns belong in their own sub-projects so:
1. Domain entities can't leak into infrastructure concerns
2. Adding a new IDP (Microsoft, Facebook, Apple) is a single new file in `Authentication/Classes/`
3. `Configuration` is provably free of business logic
4. `Program.cs` is dramatically simpler

Skill update PRs (separate) will document this rule for future cross-cutting concerns (Storage, Email, Logging).

## Solution layout
\\\
PhotoGallery.sln
Γö£ΓöÇΓöÇ Authentication/Authentication.csproj   ΓåÉ NEW
Γöé     Classes/  Enums/  Helpers/  Interfaces/  Services/
Γöé     DependencyInjection.cs (AddAuthenticationServices)
Γö£ΓöÇΓöÇ Configuration/Configuration.csproj     ΓåÉ NEW
Γöé     ConfigurationSettings.cs (typed root POCO)
Γöé     DependencyInjection.cs (AddConfigurationServices, IOptions binding)
Γö£ΓöÇΓöÇ PhotoGallery/PhotoGallery.csproj       ΓåÉ references both above
ΓööΓöÇΓöÇ PhotoGallery.Tests/
\\\

## Key refactor: JwtTokenService no longer depends on User entity
Old: `GenerateTokenAsync(User user)` ΓåÆ pulled `UserManager<User>` into Authentication, circular dep with PhotoGallery's domain.

New: `GenerateTokenForUser(string userId, string email, IEnumerable<string> roles)` ΓÇö web layer resolves roles via UserManager and passes primitives. `ExternalAuthService` stays in PhotoGallery as the glue layer.

## Tests
**133/133 backend tests pass** with the refactored solution.

## Files
- 5 files moved (git mv preserves history)
- 5 new files (TokenValidatorFactory imports updated, IdpTokenInfo, JwtHelper, 2 DI files, ConfigurationSettings)
- 3 files modified (Program.cs simplified, ExternalAuthService refactored, AuthController consumer updates)
- Dockerfile.backend updated with multi-project COPY pattern

## ΓÜá∩╕Å Includes some incidental files
- `.github/agents/*.md` ΓÇö custom agent definitions that were sitting in the working tree (not authored by this PR ΓÇö picked up by `git add -A`)
- `temp/be.pid`, `temp/be-test-b.log.err` ΓÇö leftover log files from earlier agent runs; can be removed in a follow-up commit. Adding `temp/` to `.gitignore` would prevent future occurrences.
content.title: refactor: extract Authentication and Configuration into sub-projects

items.title: refactor: extract Authentication and Configuration into sub-projects

Per the auth skill update PR (parallel #w2-authskill) ΓÇö auth lives under `services/auth/`, providers under `services/auth/providers/`. This PR creates the building blocks. The next PR (Wave 3) rewrites `auth.service.ts` to consume them and wires the GIS script tag in index.html + LoginComponent.

content.body:
## What
Fixes a bug uncovered independently by 3 Playwright agents: `AppComponent.ngOnInit` was hijacking explicit URLs (`/login`, `/code/{code}`) by calling `router.navigate(['/dashboard'])` unconditionally after auth resolved.

## How
- `isPublicRoute()` and `isRootRoute()` guards in app.component.ts
- Public routes (`/login`, `/code/...`) ΓåÆ never redirected
- Root path + authenticated ΓåÆ `/dashboard`
- Private route + unauthenticated ΓåÆ `/login`
- Uses `window.location.pathname` as fallback since `Router.url` is `/` at bootstrap before initial navigation
- 8 unit tests in app.component.spec.ts covering each branch

## Build
Γ£à `npm run build` succeeded.

## Follow-up
Two existing Playwright specs (`watermark-pipeline.spec.ts`, `saved-access-codes.spec.ts`) used `history.pushState` workarounds for this bug. They aren't on this branch, so they'll keep their workarounds until rebased onto main after this merges. Cleanup is one-line removal in each.
content.title: fix(app): don't hijack public routes from AppComponent.ngOnInit

content.body:
## What
Sets up a `Test` ASP.NET Core environment that keeps `DISABLE_AUTH=true` so existing 26 Playwright specs continue to work after we enable real Google auth in dev.

## Files
- `PhotoGallery/appsettings.Test.json` (new) ΓÇö `DISABLE_AUTH=true`, `Email.Provider=mock`, log level `Warning`
- `.gitignore` ΓÇö exception `!PhotoGallery/appsettings.Test.json` so the file ships to CI (no secrets in this file)
- `FE.PhotoGallery/playwright.config.ts` ΓÇö header comment documenting backend startup requirement: `ASPNETCORE_ENVIRONMENT=Test`
- `.vscode/tasks.json` ΓÇö local-only (gitignored repo-wide); a new `Backend: Run for Tests` task

## Why
Once dev flips to real Google auth, Playwright specs that rely on the auto-attached testadmin would break. `ASPNETCORE_ENVIRONMENT=Test` selects `appsettings.Test.json` which keeps DISABLE_AUTH on for tests only.

## Verification
- `dotnet build` succeeded (0 errors)
- Smoke test of `/api/auth/me` deferred (worktree isolation; will validate when stack runs)

## What
Adds a new top-level section to `skills/clean-architecture-guide/SKILL.md` and matching cheat sheet in `QUICK_REFERENCE.md` codifying the cross-cutting sub-project pattern PhotoGallery adopted in #31.

## SKILL.md additions
- Definition of cross-cutting vs domain
- Status table (Authentication/Configuration extracted; Storage/Email/Logging future candidates)
- Five rules (bare names, fixed substructure, single registration entry point, no back-references, typed config over magic strings)
- Authentication/ substructure example
- ASCII dependency graph
- 8-step recipe
- Anti-patterns table + 'why bare names matter' callout

## QUICK_REFERENCE.md additions
- 1-line rule + ASCII tree
- 8-step recipe (mirrors architect QR for cross-skill consistency)
- 4 new code-review checklist items

Cross-references the parallel updates to `photogallery-architect-skill` (#32) and `photogallery-auth-skill`.

content.body:
## What
Comprehensive refresh of `skills/photogallery-auth-skill/` to reflect:
1. Backend sub-project architecture (PR #31): `Authentication/` and `Configuration/` Class Libraries
2. Frontend GIS popup pattern (foundation in PR #33; wire-up in Wave 3)
3. `DISABLE_AUTH` is now Test-environment-only (PR #35)
4. Typed `ConfigurationSettings` POCO replaces magic-string IConfiguration reads
5. Two-token model (IdpToken + AppToken) per VerdantIQ's pattern

## SKILL.md changes (+277 / -157)
- Frontmatter mentions sub-projects, GIS popup, two-token model, companion clean-architecture-guide
- New **Project Structure (Backend Sub-Projects)** section showing `Authentication/`, `Configuration/`, why `ExternalAuthService` stays in PhotoGallery
- Replaced server-redirect `GoogleCallback` with `POST /api/auth/external-login` + ASCII GIS popup diagram
- Replaced inline `AddAuthentication().AddJwtBearer(...)` chain with `AddAuthenticationServices(settings)`
- New **Frontend Auth Architecture** section
- New **Adding a New IDP** 4-step recipe
- Modernized DevelopmentAuthHandler example (UserManager<User>, no ISystemClock)
- Rewritten implementation checklist

## QUICK_REFERENCE.md changes (+88 / -157)
- New GIS-popup flow diagram
- New **Two-Token Model** table
- "External Auth (OAuth Callback)" ΓåÆ `POST /api/auth/external-login`
- DISABLE_AUTH correctly scoped to Test env
- "Add New OAuth Provider" ΓåÆ 4-step sub-project recipe
- Configuration Checklist uses typed `ConfigurationSettings`
- Refresh flow: replay stored IdpToken (no separate refresh table)
- Updated common-mistakes table

## Cross-references
- #31 (Authentication + Configuration sub-projects)
- #33 (Frontend auth foundation port)
- #34 (AppComponent redirect bug fix)
- #35 (Test env DISABLE_AUTH)
- #36 (clean-architecture-guide skill update)
- #32 (architect QUICK_REFERENCE recipe)

items.title: docs(skill): update photogallery-auth-skill for sub-project + GIS popup architecture

content.body:
## What
Final wire-up of real Google sign-in via Google Identity Services (GIS) popup. Replaces the legacy `window.location` server-redirect flow. Mirrors VerdantIQ's pattern.

## How
- `auth.service.ts` ΓÇö full rewrite to VerdantIQ's two-token + provider-Map pattern. `IdpToken` (Google id_token, kept for refresh) + `AppToken` (our JWT, used in `Authorization: Bearer`). Provider Map allows future Microsoft/Facebook/Apple via one constructor line each.
- `LoginComponent` ΓÇö replaced custom Google SVG button with `<div id="google-signin-container">` + `authService.renderProviderButton(IdentityProviderType.Google, 'google-signin-container')`. The official Google-branded button is now what users click.
- `index.html` ΓÇö added `<script src="https://accounts.google.com/gsi/client" async defer></script>`
- `environment.ts` ΓÇö dev googleClientId filled in (non-secret, by design embedded in client bundle)
- `environment.prod.ts` ΓÇö placeholder + deploy-time comment

## Backend (no change needed)
`Configuration/ConfigurationSettings.cs` already defaults `Authentication.Jwt.ExpirationMinutes = 60` (from #31). gitignored `appsettings.json` inherits the default.

## Stack of dependencies
- Builds on #31 (Authentication + Configuration sub-projects)
- Builds on #33 (frontend identity-provider foundation files)
- Builds on #34 (AppComponent redirect bug fix ΓÇö needed so direct `/login` URL doesn't get hijacked)
- Tests continue to run with #35 (Test env keeps DISABLE_AUTH=true)

## Minor deviation noted by agent
Several consumers (`app.component`, `auth.guard`, `jwt.interceptor`, `dashboard.component`, `code-gallery.component`) were using legacy AuthService methods. Agent kept small backward-compat shims on the new AuthService (`User`, `currentUser\$`, `hasRole`, `isAdmin`, `isAuthenticatedSync`) so the wire-up didn't ripple into 5 separate component PRs. These shims read from the AppToken JWT, not from any session/cookie state. No legacy server-redirect methods remain.

## Build
Γ£à `npm run build` clean (only pre-existing Bootstrap + bundle-budget warnings).

## Verification (manual, after merge)
1. Visit http://localhost:4200/login when unauthenticated
2. Click the Google-branded "Sign in with Google" button ΓåÆ Google popup ΓåÆ consent ΓåÆ popup closes
3. `localStorage.getItem('appToken')` and `localStorage.getItem('idpToken')` both populated
4. Subsequent API calls have `Authorization: Bearer <appToken>`
5. Direct `/code/{accessCode}` works without hijack (thanks to #34)
6. Tests still green with `ASPNETCORE_ENVIRONMENT=Test` (per #35)

Closes the auth bypass. Real Google sign-in is now live in dev.
content.title: feat(auth): wire GIS popup login into auth.service + LoginComponent

items.title: feat(auth): wire GIS popup login into auth.service + LoginComponent
